title: Daily Briefing — Jun 09
headline: Market breadth narrowing, tech still leading, but defensive signals are creeping in—patience is king.
body: Right, so the market's bounced off lows with SPY holding $739.22, but price remains below the daily 20 MA ($746.37). Not chasing this. Volatility is ticking up (VIX 18.92 > SMA20) and Fear & Greed has cratered from 67 to 39—what was greed last month is now solid fear. Flows aren’t showing panic, but momentum is waning.

Sector rotation is classic: XLK (Tech) still leads (RS 120.4), while laggards stack up in Energy, Utilities, Staples, and Healthcare—risk levels are high across those. Financials (XLF $51.97) are improving; keep them on radar as the canary. Industrials and Discretionary are rotating up, but XLI ($173.63) just barely above the 20 MA ($173.08)—watch for confirmation.

Contrarian watch: Most big tech names (AAPL, MSFT, META, GOOGL) are all below their 20 MAs and flashing bearish bias—per rule R006, treat daily closes under 20 MA as exit signals for longs. Fear is climbing, but we aren't at extreme. Record tech outperformance and lagging breadth hint at late-cycle action (think early 2022 analogs). Don’t break something simple—a mean reversion to the weekly 20 MA zones could be on deck.
callout_body:
- SPY: $746.37 (20 MA), $715.39 (50 MA), $682.10 (200 MA)
- XLK: $183.24 (20 MA support), RS 120.4 — king of leadership
- XLF: $51.97 ready to confirm improving sector, watch weekly close
- AAPL: $304.66 (20 MA), closing below = exit longs
- VIX: 18.92 rising above SMA20; risk-on fades
- Fear & Greed: 39.4 — not extreme, but patience required
action_items: Reduce exposure on any watchlist name closing below its daily 20 MA—flagged across AAPL, MSFT, META, GOOGL, and AMZN
Monitor XLF (Financials) for follow-through; improving momentum could signal rotational opportunity if 20 MA holds
closing_quote: If it's in the press, it's in the price—follow the flows, not the headlines.
